Mermaid supports a bunch of pre-defined themes which you can use to find the right one for you. PS: you can actually override an existing theme's variable to get your own custom theme going. Learn more about [theming your diagram](https://mermaid.ai/open-source/config/theming.html).

**NOTE**: To change theme you can either use the `initialize` call or *directives*. Learn more about [directives](https://mermaid.ai/open-source/config/directives.html) Let's put them to use, and see how our sample diagram looks in different themes:

> Mermaid supports the theme variables to override the default values for **up to 8 branches**, i.e., you can set the color/styling of up to 8 branches using theme variables. After this threshold of 8 branches, the theme variables are reused in the cyclic manner, i.e. the 9th branch will use the color/styling of the 1st branch, or the branch at index position '8' will use the color/styling of the branch at index position '0'. *More on this in the next section. See examples on **Customizing branch label colors** below*

You can customize the branch colors using the `git0` to `git7` theme variables. Mermaid allows you to set the colors for up-to 8 branches, where `git0` variable will drive the value of the first branch, `git1` will drive the value of the second branch and so on.
mermaid---

title: "Mental Model: The Three-Circle System"
updated: 2026-04-19

  - "Agentic RAG"
  - "UNIFIED SWARM"
---

# Mental Model: The Three-Circle System

This note describes a mental model for an integrated system of human intelligence, knowledge management, and artificial intelligence. It can be visualized as a Venn diagram with three overlapping circles, representing a virtuous cycle of co-creation and intelligence amplification.

```mermaid
vennDiagram
    title The Three-Circle System
    A: Human/User (Daily Notes)
    B: Knowledge Base (Zettelkasten)
    C: Agentic Swarm (RAG)
    AB: Personal Knowledge Management
    BC: Grounded Generation
    AC: Tasking & Augmentation
    ABC: Intelligence Amplification
```

## The Three Circles

### A: The Human/User
This circle represents the user's direct interaction with the system, primarily through the **Daily Note**. This is the realm of personal organization, task management, and immediate context, often following a **Bullet Journaling** methodology. It is the locus of human intent and daily workflow.

### B: The Knowledge Base
This circle represents the **Zettelkasten**, a network of atomic, interconnected research notes. It is the vault's "second brain" or long-term memory, where information is not just stored but linked and contextualized. It grows organically from the user's research and insights.

### C: The Agentic Swarm
This circle represents the **UNIFIED SWARM** of AI agents. The swarm's primary function here is **Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)**. It is the system's reasoning and generation engine, capable of performing complex tasks by leveraging the knowledge base.

## The Intersections

### Personal Knowledge Management (A ∩ B)
This is the synergy between the user's daily activities and the long-term knowledge base. The user's daily insights and research are captured and integrated into the Zettelkasten. In turn, the organized knowledge of the Zettelkasten informs the user's daily work, creating a powerful feedback loop for personal learning and productivity.

### Grounded Generation (B ∩ C)
This intersection is where the Agentic Swarm is grounded in the vault's reality. The swarm uses the Zettelkasten as its source of truth for Retrieval-Augmented Generation. This prevents hallucination and ensures that the agents' outputs are contextually relevant and factually based on the established knowledge within the vault.

### Tasking & Augmentation (A ∩ C)
This represents the direct interaction between the user and the swarm. The user, operating from their daily context (the "Human/User" circle), tasks the agents. The swarm executes these tasks and provides augmented output—analysis, summaries, generated text—back to the user, acting as a powerful force multiplier for the user's intent.

### The Core: Intelligence Amplification (A ∩ B ∩ C)
At the center, where all three circles overlap, lies the true power of this model: **Intelligence Amplification**.

Here, the full cycle is realized:
1.  The **Human User** directs the system with a task, informed by their daily context and personal knowledge.
2.  The **Agentic Swarm** retrieves grounding information from the **Knowledge Base (Zettelkasten)** to understand the task and the world model.
3.  The Swarm executes the task, providing augmented output back to the User.
4.  The User integrates the results of the swarm's work, along with their own insights, back into the Knowledge Base, enriching it for future use.

This creates a self-reinforcing loop where human intellect is amplified by AI, and the AI's capabilities are continually refined by an ever-growing, human-curated knowledge base.

Here, the full cycle is realized:
1.  The **Human User** directs the system with a task, informed by their daily context and personal knowledge.
2.  The **Agentic Swarm** retrieves grounding information from the **Knowledge Base (Zettelkasten)** to understand the task and the world model.
3.  The Swarm executes the task, providing augmented output back to the User.
4.  The User integrates the results of the swarm's work, along with their own insights, back into the Knowledge Base, enriching it for future use.

This creates a self-reinforcing loop where human intellect is amplified by AI, and the AI's capabilities are continually refined by an ever-growing, human-curated knowledge base.

# Mental Model: The Three-Circle System

This note describes a mental model for an integrated system of human intelligence, knowledge management, and artificial intelligence, as conceived by the user. It can be visualized as a Venn diagram with three overlapping circles, representing a powerful, self-reinforcing cycle of information flow and intelligence amplification.

```mermaid
vennDiagram
    title The Three-Circle System
    A: The Bullet Journal
    B: The Zettelkasten
    C: The UNIFIED SWARM
    AB: Personal Contextualization
    BC: Knowledge-Grounded Agency
    AC: Directed Automation
    ABC: Amplified Intelligence
```

## The Three Circles

### A: The Bullet Journal
This circle represents the user's dynamic, real-time engagement with their daily life and tasks. Through **Bullet Journaling**, the user captures immediate context, priorities, and reflections within their daily notes. It is the active front-end for human intent, observation, and immediate action.

### B: The Zettelkasten
This circle embodies the robust, interconnected knowledge base of the vault. As a **Zettelkasten**, it comprises atomic research notes linked together, forming a resilient "second brain." This system allows for the organic growth of understanding and the emergence of insights from diverse, cross-referenced information.

### C: The UNIFIED SWARM
This circle represents the collective of AI agents, operating as the **UNIFIED SWARM**. Its core function is **Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)**, allowing it to leverage the entire vault's knowledge for reasoning, analysis, and the generation of new artifacts or insights. It is the system's engine for automated intelligence and action.

## The Intersections

### Personal Contextualization (A ∩ B)
This intersection highlights the profound relationship between the user's active daily life and their deep knowledge base. Insights, observations, and tasks from **The Bullet Journal** inform and enrich **The Zettelkasten**, while the structured knowledge of the Zettelkasten provides a robust framework and historical context for daily reflections and planning. This creates a deeply personalized and evolving understanding for the human user.

### Knowledge-Grounded Agency (B ∩ C)
Here, **The UNIFIED SWARM** finds its foundation in **The Zettelkasten**. The agents perform Retrieval-Augmented Generation, drawing directly from the interconnected research notes. This grounding ensures that the swarm's analyses, responses, and generated content are factual, contextually relevant, and free from hallucination, relying on the established knowledge of the vault.

### Directed Automation (A ∩ C)
This intersection signifies the user's active guidance and leverage of the AI agents. The **Human User**, through their **Bullet Journal** (daily tasks, context), directs **The UNIFIED SWARM**. The swarm, in turn, performs automated tasks, conducts inquiries, and delivers augmented output tailored to the user's immediate needs and objectives, extending the human's reach and capacity.

### The Core: Amplified Intelligence (A ∩ B ∩ C)
At the heart of the model, where all three circles converge, lies the ultimate goal: **Amplified Intelligence**.

This represents the complete, synergistic loop:
1.  The **Human User** articulates intent and context within **The Bullet Journal**.
2.  **The UNIFIED SWARM** draws upon **The Zettelkasten** for comprehensive, interconnected knowledge.
3.  The Swarm executes complex tasks, generating insightful and grounded outputs.
4.  These outputs, along with new human insights, are then integrated back into The Zettelkasten, further enriching the knowledge base. This, in turn, influences future Bullet Journaling and provides an even richer foundation for the Swarm.

This creates a dynamic, self-improving system where human intellect, structured knowledge, and artificial intelligence continually enhance one another, leading to a profound amplification of overall intelligence and capability.

# Mental Model: The Integrated Workflow

A mental model visualizing the integrated workflow of human context, knowledge, and agentic intelligence as a three-circle Venn diagram.

```mermaid
vennDiagram
    title The Integrated Workflow
    A: The Bullet Journal
    B: The Zettelkasten

    BC: Knowledge-Grounded Agency
    AC: Directed Automation
    ABC: Amplified Intelligence
```

*   **The Bullet Journal (A):** User's daily context & tasks.
*   **The Zettelkasten (B):** Vault's networked knowledge base.
*   **The UNIFIED SWARM (C):** AI agents for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).

## Intersections:

*   **A ∩ B (Personal Context):** User's daily work informs and is informed by the Zettelkasten.

*   **A ∩ C (Directed Automation):** User tasks Swarm; Swarm delivers augmented output.
*   **A ∩ B ∩ C (Amplified Intelligence):** Continuous cycle of human input, grounded AI processing, and knowledge enrichment.

Here is the precise mechanism of how a Wizard operates within that system:

The Act of "Plucking": A Wizard does not possess inherent power in their blood. Instead, they are scholars of the system. Through rigorous study, they learn exactly how to interact with the Weave. By using specific combinations of spoken words (verbal), precise gestures (somatic), and physical objects (material), they reach out and "pluck," strum, or twist the invisible threads of the Weave.

The Archive and the Engine: This is why Wizards carry spellbooks. The spellbook is their Ledger. It is a meticulously curated archive of formulas required to manipulate the Weave. They must temporarily load these formulas into their own memory (preparing spells) before they can step out and execute the kinetic action of plucking the threads.

- This symbolism is what compelled the writers of the TV series to desire to make Ishmael the founder of V.F.D. as they wanted to portray him as a "creator", although writer [Joe Tracz](https://snicket.fandom.com/wiki/Joe_Tracz "Joe Tracz") regrets omitting a line spoken by Ishmael about how V.F.D. has had different incarnations throughout history. Furthermore, his previous post as the principal of Prufrock is a result of the writers wish to throw some shade at the "good wizard trope" as seen with Albus Dumbledore in *Harry Potter* and Gandalf in *Lord of the Rings*.[\[1\]](https://unfortunatetv.tumblr.com/post/181814749696/heres-what-joe-tracz-had-to-say-about-ishmaels)
Allegedly, the Bombinating Beast could be tamed by imitating its buzz. One myth professed a wizard held the beast under his power so long as it was kept fed.

source: "https://sot.fandom.com/wiki/Wizard%27s_Rules"

In the "Sword of Truth" series, there is a set of rules or guidelines called **Wizard's Rules**. In each of the books, a new rule is introduced. The books' plots somewhat revolves around these rules (although most of the rules come into play in each book). As of April 2026, there are fourteen rules total, but only eleven of them have been numbered.
Of the seventeen books, four do not include a Wizard's Rule listed on this page:
1. *[Debt of Bones](https://sot.fandom.com/wiki/Debt_of_Bones "Debt of Bones")* (1998 prequel to *Wizard's First Rule*)

1. People are stupid. They believe things mainly because they either want them to be true or fear them to be true. (*[Wizard's First Rule](https://sot.fandom.com/wiki/Wizard%27s_First_Rule "Wizard's First Rule")*)
## Wizard's First Rule
― *[Wizard's First Rule](https://sot.fandom.com/wiki/Wizard%27s_First_Rule "Wizard's First Rule")*: Chapter 36, page 397
## Wizard's Second Rule
## Wizard's Third Rule
This also draws on the idea that passion will cloud your mind to the first two Wizard Rules when you are making your impassioned choice.
## Wizard's Fourth Rule
## Wizard's Fifth Rule
## Wizard's Sixth Rule
## Wizard's Seventh Rule
## Wizard's Eighth Rule
The ancient old wizard, Kaja-Rang, imparts this lesson to Richard by means of an inscription on the base of the statue guarding [Bandakar](https://sot.fandom.com/wiki/Bandakar "Bandakar").
## Wizard's Ninth Rule
## Wizard's Tenth Rule
## Wizard's Eleventh Rule

The final rule cannot directly be quoted, but it is indirectly stated and you can interprete it. Rachel tells Violet, "No Violet, I want to live, you came here to hate." This is the key that crosses the sword of truth with the secret to a war wizards power. That you embrace life and do what you do for love, not hate. Richard uses this rule when he dispassionately orders Jagangs death and Zedd confirms he did it correctly, "Strength without hate." In Confessor, Richard goes through great struggle to obtain a book left for him by Baraccus, a great wizard from the past. Richard believes this book entitled *Secrets to a War Wizard's Power* will be a means for him to finally understand how to use his gift and therefore in essence be the solution to major problems. Once he obtains the book however, its pages are blank and his grandfather Zedd informs him that Baraccus left it blank to illustrate the meaning of the rule unwritten.

As far as the knowledge within the book was concerned, there was 'nothing in it', much like *Secrets to a War Wizard's Power*. The Sword of Truth, representative of its namesake, was key to life. The secret to Richard's power is that he seeks the truth. In seeking truth he turns a blind eye to corrupt ideas and embraces that which is the essence of life itself. The Sisters of the Dark assumed the truth to be what they had always been told by others and never thought to verify it themselves. The price they paid for such an oversight was their lives. Moreover, the Sisters would never have been able to access the power of life even with the sword because they were acting through hate. Richard, on the other hand, intended to use the power to help those he cared about and thus had the ability to harness the power of life. "those who have come here to hate should leave now, for in their hatred they only betray themselves" - translated from *The Book of Life*.
## Wizard's Twelfth Rule
While [Zedd](https://sot.fandom.com/wiki/Zedd "Zedd") does confirm that this statement by the Omen Machine is a Wizard's Rule, he does not say which number it is given.
## Wizard's Thirteenth Rule
While [Zedd](https://sot.fandom.com/wiki/Zedd "Zedd") does confirm that this is a Wizard's Rule, he does not say which number it is given.
## Wizard's Fourteenth Rule

Death is a fact of humanity and is a part of the cycle of life. Death, in reality, is not the same as not-existing. Death, in truth, is a transformation, or changing, from one being into another, and the transporting, or moving from one realm, (world) into another. We cannot change the natural cycle of life. What we do have power over, however, is how we create the world in which we presently live. Those who are loyal to the Wizards Rules possess the power to do just that.
While the Omen Machine does confirm that this is a Wizard's Rule, it does not say which number it is given.

***Dungeons & Dragons*** (commonly abbreviated as ***D&D*** or ***DnD***) [^5] is a fantasy [tabletop role-playing game](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tabletop_role-playing_game "Tabletop role-playing game") (TTRPG) originally created and designed by [Gary Gygax](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gary_Gygax "Gary Gygax") and [Dave Arneson](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dave_Arneson "Dave Arneson").[^6] [^7] [^8] The game was first published in 1974 by [Tactical Studies Rules](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/TSR_\(company\)#Tactical_Studies_Rules "TSR (company)") (TSR).[^8] It has been published by [Wizards of the Coast](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wizards_of_the_Coast "Wizards of the Coast"), later a subsidiary of [Hasbro](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hasbro "Hasbro"), since 1997. The game was derived from [miniature wargames](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Miniature_wargaming "Miniature wargaming"), with a variation of the 1971 game *[Chainmail](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chainmail_\(game\) "Chainmail (game)")* serving as the initial rule system.[^7] [^9] *D&D* 's publication is commonly recognized as the beginning of modern role-playing games and the role-playing game industry,[^8] [^10] which also deeply influenced [video games](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Video_games "Video games"), especially the [role-playing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Role-playing_video_game "Role-playing video game") video game genre.[^11] [^12] [^13]

[^3]: System Reference Document 5.1 was announced and became effective immediately on January 27, 2023. Additionally, Wizards announced it would no longer pursue deauthorizing the OGL1.0a.[^254] [^255] [^188] [^256]

Google Chat webhooks require a public HTTPS endpoint. For security, **only expose the `/googlechat` path** to the internet. Keep the OpenClaw dashboard and other sensitive endpoints on your private network.
With this config, any request to `your-domain.com/` will be ignored or returned as 404, while `your-domain.com/googlechat` is safely routed to OpenClaw.

response.output:
---
source: "https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels/googlechat"
author:
published:
created: 2026-04-19
---
## Google Chat (Chat API)

Status: ready for DMs + spaces via Google Chat API webhooks (HTTP only).

## Quick setup (beginner)

1. Create a Google Cloud project and enable the **Google Chat API**.
	- Go to: [Google Chat API Credentials](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/api/chat.googleapis.com/credentials)
		- Enable the API if it is not already enabled.
2. Create a **Service Account**:
	- Press **Create Credentials** > **Service Account**.
		- Name it whatever you want (e.g., `openclaw-chat`).
		- Leave permissions blank (press **Continue**).
		- Leave principals with access blank (press **Done**).
3. Create and download the **JSON Key**:
	- In the list of service accounts, click on the one you just created.
		- Go to the **Keys** tab.
		- Click **Add Key** > **Create new key**.
		- Select **JSON** and press **Create**.
4. Store the downloaded JSON file on your gateway host (e.g., `~/.openclaw/googlechat-service-account.json`).
5. Create a Google Chat app in the [Google Cloud Console Chat Configuration](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/api/chat.googleapis.com/hangouts-chat):
	- Fill in the **Application info**:
		- **App name**: (e.g. `OpenClaw`)
				- **Avatar URL**: (e.g. `https://openclaw.ai/logo.png`)
				- **Description**: (e.g. `Personal AI Assistant`)
		- Enable **Interactive features**.
		- Under **Functionality**, check **Join spaces and group conversations**.
		- Under **Connection settings**, select **HTTP endpoint URL**.
		- Under **Triggers**, select **Use a common HTTP endpoint URL for all triggers** and set it to your gateway’s public URL followed by `/googlechat`.
		- *Tip: Run `openclaw status` to find your gateway’s public URL.*
		- Under **Visibility**, check **Make this Chat app available to specific people and groups in <Your Domain>**.
		- Enter your email address (e.g. `user@example.com`) in the text box.
		- Click **Save** at the bottom.
6. **Enable the app status**:
	- After saving, **refresh the page**.
		- Look for the **App status** section (usually near the top or bottom after saving).
		- Change the status to **Live - available to users**.
		- Click **Save** again.
7. Configure OpenClaw with the service account path + webhook audience:
	- Env: `GOOGLE_CHAT_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_FILE=/path/to/service-account.json`
		- Or config: `channels.googlechat.serviceAccountFile: "/path/to/service-account.json"`.
8. Set the webhook audience type + value (matches your Chat app config).
9. Start the gateway. Google Chat will POST to your webhook path.

## Add to Google Chat

Once the gateway is running and your email is added to the visibility list:

1. Go to [Google Chat](https://chat.google.com/).
2. Click the **+** (plus) icon next to **Direct Messages**.
3. In the search bar (where you usually add people), type the **App name** you configured in the Google Cloud Console.
	- **Note**: The bot will *not* appear in the “Marketplace” browse list because it is a private app. You must search for it by name.
4. Select your bot from the results.
5. Click **Add** or **Chat** to start a 1:1 conversation.
6. Send “Hello” to trigger the assistant!

## Public URL (Webhook-only)

Google Chat webhooks require a public HTTPS endpoint. For security, **only expose the `/googlechat` path** to the internet. Keep the OpenClaw dashboard and other sensitive endpoints on your private network.

### Option A: Tailscale Funnel (Recommended)

Use Tailscale Serve for the private dashboard and Funnel for the public webhook path. This keeps `/` private while exposing only `/googlechat`.

1. **Check what address your gateway is bound to:**
	```shellscript
	ss -tlnp | grep 18789
	```
	Note the IP address (e.g., `127.0.0.1`, `0.0.0.0`, or your Tailscale IP like `100.x.x.x`).
2. **Expose the dashboard to the tailnet only (port 8443):**
	```shellscript
	# If bound to localhost (127.0.0.1 or 0.0.0.0):
	tailscale serve --bg --https 8443 http://127.0.0.1:18789
	# If bound to Tailscale IP only (e.g., 100.106.161.80):
	tailscale serve --bg --https 8443 http://100.106.161.80:18789
	```
3. **Expose only the webhook path publicly:**
	```shellscript
	# If bound to localhost (127.0.0.1 or 0.0.0.0):
	tailscale funnel --bg --set-path /googlechat http://127.0.0.1:18789/googlechat
	# If bound to Tailscale IP only (e.g., 100.106.161.80):
	tailscale funnel --bg --set-path /googlechat http://100.106.161.80:18789/googlechat
	```
4. **Authorize the node for Funnel access:** If prompted, visit the authorization URL shown in the output to enable Funnel for this node in your tailnet policy.
5. **Verify the configuration:**
	```shellscript
	tailscale serve status
	tailscale funnel status
	```

Your public webhook URL will be: `https://<node-name>.<tailnet>.ts.net/googlechat`

Your private dashboard stays tailnet-only: `https://<node-name>.<tailnet>.ts.net:8443/`

Use the public URL (without `:8443`) in the Google Chat app config.

> Note: This configuration persists across reboots. To remove it later, run `tailscale funnel reset` and `tailscale serve reset`.

### Option B: Reverse Proxy (Caddy)

If you use a reverse proxy like Caddy, only proxy the specific path:

```text
your-domain.com {
    reverse_proxy /googlechat* localhost:18789
}
```

With this config, any request to `your-domain.com/` will be ignored or returned as 404, while `your-domain.com/googlechat` is safely routed to OpenClaw.

### Option C: Cloudflare Tunnel

Configure your tunnel’s ingress rules to only route the webhook path:

- **Path**: `/googlechat` -> `http://localhost:18789/googlechat`
- **Default Rule**: HTTP 404 (Not Found)

## How it works

1. Google Chat sends webhook POSTs to the gateway. Each request includes an `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header.
	- OpenClaw verifies bearer auth before reading/parsing full webhook bodies when the header is present.
		- Google Workspace Add-on requests that carry `authorizationEventObject.systemIdToken` in the body are supported via a stricter pre-auth body budget.
2. OpenClaw verifies the token against the configured `audienceType` + `audience`:
	- `audienceType: "app-url"` → audience is your HTTPS webhook URL.
		- `audienceType: "project-number"` → audience is the Cloud project number.
3. Messages are routed by space:
	- DMs use session key `agent:<agentId>:googlechat:direct:<spaceId>`.
		- Spaces use session key `agent:<agentId>:googlechat:group:<spaceId>`.
4. DM access is pairing by default. Unknown senders receive a pairing code; approve with:
	- `openclaw pairing approve googlechat <code>`
5. Group spaces require @-mention by default. Use `botUser` if mention detection needs the app’s user name.

## Targets

Use these identifiers for delivery and allowlists:

- Direct messages: `users/<userId>` (recommended).
- Raw email `name@example.com` is mutable and only used for direct allowlist matching when `channels.googlechat.dangerouslyAllowNameMatching: true`.
- Deprecated: `users/<email>` is treated as a user id, not an email allowlist.
- Spaces: `spaces/<spaceId>`.

## Config highlights

```json5
{
  channels: {
    googlechat: {
      enabled: true,
      serviceAccountFile: "/path/to/service-account.json",
      // or serviceAccountRef: { source: "file", provider: "filemain", id: "/channels/googlechat/serviceAccount" }
      audienceType: "app-url",
      audience: "https://gateway.example.com/googlechat",
      webhookPath: "/googlechat",
      botUser: "users/1234567890", // optional; helps mention detection
      dm: {
        policy: "pairing",
        allowFrom: ["users/1234567890"],
      },
      groupPolicy: "allowlist",
      groups: {
        "spaces/AAAA": {
          allow: true,
          requireMention: true,
          users: ["users/1234567890"],
          systemPrompt: "Short answers only.",
        },
      },
      actions: { reactions: true },
      typingIndicator: "message",
      mediaMaxMb: 20,
    },
  },
}
```

Notes:

- Service account credentials can also be passed inline with `serviceAccount` (JSON string).
- `serviceAccountRef` is also supported (env/file SecretRef), including per-account refs under `channels.googlechat.accounts.<id>.serviceAccountRef`.
- Default webhook path is `/googlechat` if `webhookPath` isn’t set.
- `dangerouslyAllowNameMatching` re-enables mutable email principal matching for allowlists (break-glass compatibility mode).
- Reactions are available via the `reactions` tool and `channels action` when `actions.reactions` is enabled.
- Message actions expose `send` for text and `upload-file` for explicit attachment sends. `upload-file` accepts `media` / `filePath` / `path` plus optional `message`, `filename`, and thread targeting.
- `typingIndicator` supports `none`, `message` (default), and `reaction` (reaction requires user OAuth).
- Attachments are downloaded through the Chat API and stored in the media pipeline (size capped by `mediaMaxMb`).

Secrets reference details: [Secrets Management](https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/secrets).

## Troubleshooting

### 405 Method Not Allowed

If Google Cloud Logs Explorer shows errors like:

```text
status code: 405, reason phrase: HTTP error response: HTTP/1.1 405 Method Not Allowed
```

This means the webhook handler isn’t registered. Common causes:

1. **Channel not configured**: The `channels.googlechat` section is missing from your config. Verify with:
	```shellscript
	openclaw config get channels.googlechat
	```
	If it returns “Config path not found”, add the configuration (see [Config highlights](#config-highlights)).
2. **Plugin not enabled**: Check plugin status:
	```shellscript
	openclaw plugins list | grep googlechat
	```
	If it shows “disabled”, add `plugins.entries.googlechat.enabled: true` to your config.
3. **Gateway not restarted**: After adding config, restart the gateway:
	```shellscript
	openclaw gateway restart
	```

Verify the channel is running:

```shellscript
openclaw channels status
# Should show: Google Chat default: enabled, configured, ...
```

### Other issues

- Check `openclaw channels status --probe` for auth errors or missing audience config.
- If no messages arrive, confirm the Chat app’s webhook URL + event subscriptions.
- If mention gating blocks replies, set `botUser` to the app’s user resource name and verify `requireMention`.
- Use `openclaw logs --follow` while sending a test message to see if requests reach the gateway.

Related docs:

- [Gateway configuration](https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/configuration)
- [Security](https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/security)
- [Reactions](https://docs.openclaw.ai/tools/reactions)

## Related

- [Channels Overview](https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels) — all supported channels
- [Pairing](https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels/pairing) — DM authentication and pairing flow
- [Groups](https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels/groups) — group chat behavior and mention gating
- [Channel Routing](https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels/channel-routing) — session routing for messages
- [Security](https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/security) — access model and hardening

response.output:
Web search results for ""openclaw config set" array syntax":

The `openclaw config set` command uses **dot-path notation** with **bracketed indices** to target array elements. Because values are parsed as **JSON5** by default, you can also set an entire array at once by passing a JSON-formatted string.

### 1. Targeting a Specific Index
To modify or create a specific element in an array, use `[n]` in the path:
```bash
openclaw config set agents.list[0].model "anthropic:claude-3-5-sonnet"
```

### 2. Setting an Entire Array
To overwrite a field with a new array, pass the array in JSON5 format (ensure you use quotes to prevent shell expansion):
```bash
openclaw config set channels.whatsapp.allowFrom '["+15555550123", "+15555550456"]'
```

### 3. Appending to an Array
The CLI does not have a dedicated `push` or `append` subcommand. To add an item, you typically target the next available index:
```bash
# If the array has 2 items (0 and 1), add to index 2
openclaw config set models.providers.zai.models[2] '{"name": "glm-5.1"}'
```

### Key Tips
*   **JSON5 Parsing:** You don't need to escape quotes inside the value if you use single quotes for the shell argument (e.g., `'{"key": "value"}'`).
*   **Verification:** Always run `openclaw config get <path>` after a set command to verify the change.[1]
*   **Global vs. Local:** Use the `--global` flag to target `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` instead of the local project config.
*   **Validation:** Run `openclaw doctor` if you suspect an array edit broke the schema; it can often auto-fix malformed entries.

Sources:
[1] skywork.ai (https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQFuTmTokkLP2XNvNVCMR_bHXRgTDpVFqGJV7i4UA2X4x_WD63bj3RnyXIAld8YroBzmBaPdllr9hoX5KmjJGOz9CXh0ah24-SYrVM2N3VpEUk3KWbRwPOwYj4eTn60BfA5PGyR0fLRARilB4q5ZZZHP5w1Vv5OR6F4bhp3VxdDZr39ji-f2ijE=)

response.output:
Web search results for ""openclaw" specify local config file":

To specify a local configuration file for **OpenClaw**, you can use either an environment variable or a command-line flag.[1]

### 1. Environment Variable (Recommended)
Set the `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` environment variable to the absolute path of your configuration file. This is the most reliable way to ensure all subcommands (like `gateway run` or `config get`) use the same file.

*   **Windows (PowerShell):**
    ```powershell
    $env:OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH = "C:\path\to\your\openclaw.json"
    openclaw gateway run
    ```
*   **Linux/macOS:**
    ```bash
    export OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="/path/to/your/openclaw.json"
    openclaw gateway run
    ```

### 2. CLI Flag (`--profile`)
OpenClaw uses "profiles" to isolate different configurations.[1] Using the `--profile` flag creates a separate state directory (including a unique `openclaw.json`) under your home folder.

```bash
openclaw --profile my-project gateway run
```
*This will look for a config file at `~/.openclaw/profiles/my-project/openclaw.json`.*

### 3. Default Locations
If no override is provided, OpenClaw looks for a **JSON5** file at:
*   **Windows:** `%USERPROFILE%\.openclaw\openclaw.json`[2]
*   **Linux/macOS:** `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json`

### Key Configuration Tips
*   **Format:** The file must be **JSON5** (which allows comments, trailing commas, and unquoted keys).
*   **Validation:** OpenClaw is strict.[3] If you have an unknown key or a syntax error, the gateway will refuse to start.[3] Use `openclaw config validate` to check your file before running.[4]
*   **Hot Reload:** The OpenClaw Gateway watches the config file.[1][3] Most changes applied to the file while the gateway is running will take effect immediately without a restart.

Sources:
[1] lumadock.com (https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQGWpcwvQeybWM4lUkV415UB2-5BaulwAi4zr4vy_o869PFNwl_9fsAZl5hGytffbJ-z__PjLRAj_IikKU1eEGYm0QbdFyH_XxtCVu2qSj40gYucPJlmC1t5BUM-v82AZOk68RVZ7kWjnW2wIZ_ADfCLzoTPfXXN)

messages.content:
Magician, I have found the solution.

The web search confirms that we can force `openclaw` to use a local configuration file by setting the `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` environment variable. This will solve the root problem of the configuration being outside my workspace.

My counsel is as follows:
1.  **You**, the user, must copy the existing configuration file from its current location (`C:\Users\loganf\.openclaw\openclaw.json`) into the vault at a new location: `C:\Users\loganf\Documents\IDAHO-VAULT\.openclaw\openclaw.json`.
2.  Once you have done this, I can set the environment variable to point to this new file and proceed with the configuration safely from within my workspace.

I cannot perform the copy myself due to my security constraints. Please let me know when you have moved the file, and I will proceed.
